evals.prompt: I want to set up a new nix config for my desktop (NixOS x86_64) and laptop (macOS with nix-darwin, aarch64). Both machines should share Home Manager config. Can you scaffold the basic structure?
evals.expected_output: A complete dendritic scaffold with flake.nix, flake-parts.nix, and system-default.nix. Darwin and Home Manager helpers should be uncommented. Directory structure should follow dendritic conventions.
expectations: The output includes a flake.nix with flake-parts, import-tree, home-manager, and nix-darwin inputs
The output includes a flake-parts.nix that enables flake-parts modules and defines mkNixos, mkDarwin, and mkHome helpers
The output includes a system-default.nix with flake.modules blocks for nixos, darwin, and homeManager
The directory structure follows dendritic conventions (modules/ with programs/, services/, system/, etc.)
The systems list is trimmed to only x86_64-linux and aarch64-darwin

evals.prompt: I have a dendritic nix config already set up. I need to add printing support -- it should configure CUPS on my NixOS machines and also set up printer-specific PPD paths in Home Manager for my user. The NixOS side needs to pull in the Home Manager config automatically. How should I structure this?
evals.expected_output: A Multi Context Aspect in modules/services/printing.nix that defines flake.modules.nixos.printing (with CUPS config and home-manager.sharedModules pulling in the HM module) and flake.modules.homeManager.printing (with user-level printer config).
expectations: The pattern is identified as Multi Context Aspect (system module that pulls in Home Manager config)
The module is placed in modules/services/ directory
The NixOS module uses home-manager.sharedModules to pull in the Home Manager module
Both NixOS and Home Manager configs live in the same file
The Home Manager module is referenced via inputs.self.modules.homeManager

evals.expected_output: Identifies the lib.mkIf-in-imports anti-pattern, explains that Nix evaluates imports unconditionally, and suggests using the Conditional Aspect (lib.mkMerge + lib.mkIf on config values) or restructuring so the host simply includes or excludes the import.
expectations: Correctly identifies the anti-pattern: lib.mkIf in imports does not work because Nix evaluates imports unconditionally
Suggests the Conditional Aspect pattern (lib.mkMerge + lib.mkIf on config values) as an alternative
Does NOT suggest keeping lib.mkIf in imports with any workaround
Suggests using imports as the enable toggle (add or remove the import) as the dendritic-idiomatic approach

evals.prompt: I have an existing NixOS config with 3 hosts organized by host (hosts/laptop/configuration.nix, hosts/desktop/configuration.nix, hosts/server/configuration.nix). Each host has its own big configuration.nix with SSH, printing, users, packages, all mixed together. I use specialArgs to pass inputs and a username variable around. I want to restructure this using dendritic so I can share features between hosts. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: A migration plan that follows the dendritic migration guide: add flake-parts + import-tree, wrap outputs with mkFlake, replace specialArgs with let-bindings or Constants Aspect, reorganize from host-centric to feature-centric one feature at a time.
expectations: References or follows the migration guide steps (add flake-parts/import-tree, wrap with mkFlake, replace specialArgs, reorganize)
Addresses the specialArgs issue specifically -- replace with let-bindings or Constants Aspect
Suggests incremental migration (one feature at a time, not a full rewrite)
The end-state structure organizes by feature (services/ssh.nix, services/printing.nix) not by host
